Aims                                                                                                                               Aims

   I Most problems in fluid and solid mechanics, quantum                                                                               I Most problems in fluid and solid mechanics, quantum
     mechanics, electromagnetism, and other areas of physics                                                                             mechanics, electromagnetism, and other areas of physics
     can be modelled as partial differential equations (PDEs).                                                                           can be modelled as partial differential equations (PDEs).
                                                                                                                                       I This topic introduces the method of separation of
                                                                                                                                         variables that solves the wave equation, heat equation,
                                                                                                                                         and Laplace equation.

 Aims                                                                                                                      What you need to know

   I Most problems in fluid and solid mechanics, quantum
     mechanics, electromagnetism, and other areas of physics
     can be modelled as partial differential equations (PDEs).
                                                                                                                             I Partial derivatives.
   I This topic introduces the method of separation of
     variables that solves the wave equation, heat equation,
     and Laplace equation.
   I There are many PDEs that do not have known analytical
     solutions, but can be solved approximately by numerical
     methods. MATH2089 introduces the finite difference
     method to solve PDEs.

 What is a PDE?                                                                                                             What is a PDE?

   I A partial differential equation (PDE) is an equation                                                                     I A partial differential equation (PDE) is an equation
     involving one or more partial derivatives of an unknown                                                                    involving one or more partial derivatives of an unknown
     function that depends on more than one variable. These                                                                     function that depends on more than one variable. These
     variables are often time t ∈ [0, ∞) and position ⃗x ∈ Ω for                                                                variables are often time t ∈ [0, ∞) and position ⃗x ∈ Ω for
     a spatial domain Ω ⊆ Rd where d ∈ {1, 2, 3}.                                                                               a spatial domain Ω ⊆ Rd where d ∈ {1, 2, 3}.
                                                                                                                              I The order of a PDE is the order of the highest derivative
                                                                                                                                in the equation.

What is a PDE?                                                                                                             What is a PDE?

   I A partial differential equation (PDE) is an equation                                                                     I A partial differential equation (PDE) is an equation
     involving one or more partial derivatives of an unknown                                                                    involving one or more partial derivatives of an unknown
     function that depends on more than one variable. These                                                                     function that depends on more than one variable. These
     variables are often time t ∈ [0, ∞) and position ⃗x ∈ Ω for                                                                variables are often time t ∈ [0, ∞) and position ⃗x ∈ Ω for
     a spatial domain Ω ⊆ Rd where d ∈ {1, 2, 3}.                                                                               a spatial domain Ω ⊆ Rd where d ∈ {1, 2, 3}.
   I The order of a PDE is the order of the highest derivative                                                                I The order of a PDE is the order of the highest derivative
     in the equation.                                                                                                           in the equation.
   I A PDE is linear if it is linear in the unknown function u                                                                I A PDE is linear if it is linear in the unknown function u
     and its partial derivatives.                                                                                               and its partial derivatives.
                                                                                                                              I A solution of a PDE is a function of several variables
                                                                                                                                which satisfies the equation.

Vibrating string model                                                                                                               Vibrating string model
  Consider a stretched string having the following properties:                                                                         Consider a stretched string having the following properties:
   1. the string is fixed at x = 0 and x = L;                                                                                           1. the string is fixed at x = 0 and x = L;
                                                                                                                                        2. the string is uniform, perfectly elastic and offers no
                                                                                                                                           resistance to bending;




  At position x and time t, let                                                                                                        At position x and time t, let

                    u = u(x, t) = transverse deflection,                                                                                                 u = u(x, t) = transverse deflection,
